title: Isolate per-assistant failures; refuse to clobber malformed configs
context: Old scaffolders silently corrupted user configs: discarding comments/syntax errors, creating invalid TOML duplicates, aborting entire `hunch init` on one failure. Users lost data without knowing.
decision: Three-layer defense: (1) readJsonObj throws on parse error instead of returning `{}`, (2) writeCodexConfig throws if user already defined [mcp_servers.hunch], (3) scaffoldProviders wraps each assistant in try-catch so one failure degrades to a warning, not init abort.
consequences: User config with comments/syntax errors is never overwritten — preserves their other MCP servers
init partially succeeds: assistants that can scaffold do, those that can't emit warnings
Clear error messages guide the user to fix unparseable files rather than silently losing data
Error field on ProviderScaffold surfaces failures to CLI, allowing targeted warning display
alternatives_rejected: Silently clobber (old behavior) — easy but causes data loss
Hard abort on first failure — prevents downstream assistants from scaffolding
Auto-repair unparseable configs — too risky, could introduce new bugs
Create duplicate TOML tables anyway — would corrupt the file irreversibly
